Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and enhance app.py backend code for the CarRental web application. Specifically, ensure all Flask routes correctly correspond to frontend pages and handle data reading from local text files as per the requirements. Address any minor logic improvements if needed, but focus on backend functionality and data handling.
Composition.Programmer 2: Develop and implement all missing HTML template files required by the Flask app for the CarRental web application. This includes creating templates for all nine pages  Dashboard, Vehicle Search, Vehicle Details, Booking, Insurance Options, Rental History, Reservation Management, Special Requests, and Locations. Each template must strictly follow the detailed page design and element IDs specified in the requirements document to ensure seamless integration with the backend and correct rendering without jinja2.exceptions.TemplateNotFound errors. Coordinate with Programmer 1 to confirm data passed to templates and ensure UI elements match backend data structures.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
